settings.title: Choosing the right driver.
settings.subtitle: KAYVA 480 Pro Strip runs on 24V and draws 12W per metre. Here's how to size your driver correctly.

settings.content: Multiply your strip length by 12W per metre, then add 20% headroom so the driver isn't running at full capacity. A driver running flat-out runs hot and fails early.

settings.header_row: Strip length | Power draw | Recommended driver

settings.label: For runs 6 m and longer:
settings.text: Power the strip from both ends, or split into segments with separate feeds. This prevents voltage drop, where the far end of a long run appears dimmer than the start.

settings.content: Use a 24V constant-voltage LED driver. For dimmable installations, choose a TRIAC-dimmable 24V driver and pair it with a compatible wall dimmer. KAYVA strip itself doesn't dim on its own — the driver controls dimming.

settings.label: Always use a licensed electrician
settings.text: for connecting the driver to mains power in Singapore. The low-voltage side (driver to strip) is safe to handle when unpowered.

settings.heading: Ready to order your strip?
settings.subtext: KAYVA 480 Pro Strip — Ra95+, 24V, dotless COB. Available in 2700K and 4000K.
